heading: Federal Crop Insurance Corporation; creation; offices
label: 1503 Federal Crop Insurance Corporation; creation; offices
text: To carry out the purposes of this subchapter, there is hereby created as an agency of and within the Department a body corporate with the name “Federal Crop Insurance Corporation”. The principal office of the Corporation shall be located in the District of Columbia, but there may be established agencies or branch offices elsewhere in the United States under rules and regulations prescribed by the Board.

Transfer of FunctionsUnder authority of Ex. Ord. No. 9577, , Secretary of Agriculture consolidated administration of program of Federal Crop Insurance Corporation in Production and Marketing Administration by Memorandum 1118, . 1946 Reorg. Plan No. 3, § 501, eff. , 11 F.R. 7877, , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees, transferred administration of program of Federal Crop Insurance Corporation to Secretary of Agriculture. In his letter to Congress President stated that purpose of this transfer was to permit Secretary of Agriculture to continue consolidation already effected in Production and Marketing Administration.June 29, 1945Aug. 18, 1945July 16, 194660 Stat. 1100Federal Crop Insurance Corporation consolidated with other agencies into Agricultural Conservation and Adjustment Administration for duration of war, see Ex. Ord. No. 9069.
